statement.p: To estimate the health of the black-tailed deer population in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area, the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife caught, tagged, and released 99 black-tailed deer. A month later, they returned and observed 83 black-tailed deer, 9 of which had tags. Approximately how many black-tailed deer are in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area?

solution.p: Let x represent how many deer are in the Jewell Meadow Area. We set up a proportional equation:

p.me: \begin{aligned} \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}} \amp= \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}}\\ \frac{9}{83} \amp= \frac{99}{x}\\ \frac{9}{83} \multiplyright{83\cdot x} \amp= \frac{99}{x}\multiplyright{83\cdot x}\\ 9 \cdot x \amp= 99\cdot 83\\ \divideunder{9 \cdot x}{9} \amp= \divideunder{99\cdot 83}{9}\\ x \amp= 913 \end{aligned}

solution.p: There are approximately 913 deer in the Jewell Meadow Area.
